free_text: The scattered alpha particles were counted by three
scintillation telescopes located in the image plane
Each telescope consisted of a small plastic
scintillator (40 mm high, 6 mm large) in coincidence
with a large scintillator situated behind.
This last detector was used with a discriminator
which eliminated the less ionizing particles.
